title: Application of qPCR in Detecting Adulteration in Processed Meat
author: Sophian, Alfi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Processed     meat     adulteration     remains     a     significant   global   concern  ,   undermining     food     safety  ,   religious     compliance  ,   and     consumer     trust  .   Traditional     analytical     techniques  ,   such   as   microscopy     or   ELISA, are   often     time-consuming  ,   lack     sensitivity  ,   and   fail   to     detect     low     levels     of     adulteration   in   complex     matrices  .   Quantitative     Polymerase     Chain     Reaction   (  qPCR  )   offers     a     rapid  ,   highly     sensitive  ,   and     specific     method     for     detecting     and     quantifying     species-specific   DNA in   meat     products  . By   amplifying     targeted   DNA   sequences  ,   qPCR     can     identify     even     trace     amounts     of     undeclared     animal     species     within     hours  .   Implementing     qPCR   in   food     authentication     programs     supports     regulatory     compliance  ,   protects     consumers     from     fraud  ,   and     safeguards     public     health  .   Strengthening     national     capacity     for     qPCR   testing   ensures     transparency  ,   strengthens     supply     chain     integrity  ,   and     enhances   global   trade     competitiveness  .

By   amplifying     targeted   DNA   sequences  ,   qPCR     can     identify     even     trace     amounts     of     undeclared     animal     species     within     hours  .   Implementing     qPCR   in   food     authentication     programs     supports     regulatory     compliance  ,   protects     consumers     from     fraud  ,   and     safeguards     public     health  .
